QF.id: VIII Introduction to Forwards and Futures_127

question.text: As per the SEBI circular on uniform expiry days, exchanges must choose their expiry day as:

answer.text: Either Tuesday or Thursday for all equity derivatives contracts, and this choice requires SEBI approval to change
Any day of the week at the exchange's discretion without SEBI approval
Monday for index contracts and Friday for stock contracts
The last business day of each month regardless of the day of the week

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI mandates exchanges choose either Tuesday or Thursday as the expiry day for ALL equity derivatives. NSE chose Tuesday, BSE chose Thursday. Changing the expiry day requires prior SEBI approval. **The Trap:** It's not any day — only Tuesday or Thursday. And the choice applies to ALL contracts on that exchange (monthly, weekly). **Cross-Exam Context:** Uniform expiry day rules are a recent SEBI regulatory change. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Tuesday (NSE) or Thursday (BSE). All contracts follow. SEBI approval needed to change.
